tasks.title: Capture build, content, and tuning identity
tasks.summary: Create the evidence-harness identity adapter that records immutable build, content, source, editor, package, save-schema, and tuning-profile identity.
tasks.objective: Make every Stage 1 evidence session attributable to one exact offline build and frozen tuning configuration before any gameplay implementation exists.
tasks.context: Milestone S1.0 Foundation and evidence harness; frozen product question: Can Shooter Mover provide a reproducible, rapidly resettable Stage 1 evidence environment with build identity, deterministic setup, bounded local diagnostics, benchmark combat space, a short-route shell, quick restart, and trustworthy evidence capture before gameplay systems are implemented?
inputs: Consumed contracts: ContentVersion and BuildIdentity v1 from CS-002.
Consumed foundation: Windows build entrypoint from UF-010 and prototype-debt policy from UF-011.
implementation_notes: Forbidden: accepted planning/requirements, assembly-generated task artifacts, credentials or secrets, unowned serialized assets, Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Domain/, Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/, and gameplay implementation. Keep diagnostics local/offline and bounded.
Serialized owner: none. Save impact: read identity only. Registry impact: read fingerprints only; do not generate or hand-edit registries.
Formal identity must fail closed when required values are missing, malformed, provisional, or internally inconsistent.
Rollback: remove only the four owned files and rerun identity tests. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: One deterministic record contains source commit, dirty-state policy, Unity/editor version, package lock fingerprint, content version, save schema version, build target, build configuration, and tuning-profile ID.
Two records from the same frozen inputs serialize identically; changing any required identity input changes the fingerprint.
Missing formal identity is classified as invalid evidence rather than replaced with placeholders.
verification: Automated: deterministic serialization, field completeness, dirty-source, changed-tuning, changed-package-lock, and malformed-identity tests.
Manual/playable: compare an editor capture and a UF-010 Windows build capture for the same frozen source.
proof_required: Two sample identity records showing stable and changed fingerprints
tasks.handoff_notes: EH-002 consumes this identity record; later manifest and smoke tasks must not invent a second build-identity format.

tasks.title: Define deterministic evidence configuration and seed fixture
tasks.summary: Define one versioned Stage 1 evidence configuration that binds run seed, input-intent fixture, quality profile, locale, resolution, diagnostics bounds, and identity.
tasks.objective: Provide a reproducible setup artifact that can be loaded identically in edit-mode, play-mode, and Windows smoke runs.
tasks.context: Milestone S1.0 Foundation and evidence harness; frozen product question: Can Shooter Mover provide a reproducible, rapidly resettable Stage 1 evidence environment with build identity, deterministic setup, bounded local diagnostics, benchmark combat space, a short-route shell, quick restart, and trustworthy evidence capture before gameplay systems are implemented?
inputs: Consumed contracts: Identity v1 and Player Intents v1.
Consumed output: EH-001 evidence identity capture.
implementation_notes: Forbidden: accepted planning/requirements, assembly-generated task artifacts, credentials or secrets, unowned serialized assets, Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Domain/, Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/, and gameplay implementation. Keep diagnostics local/offline and bounded.
Serialized owner: tools/evidence/fixtures/stage1-evidence-config-v1.json only. Save and registry impact: none.
The seed controls harness setup and scripted intent fixtures only; it must not imply mature gameplay randomness or balance.
Rollback: remove owned files and restore callers to explicit test defaults. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: The canonical fixture has an explicit schema/version, seed, identity reference, intent-fixture version, quality profile, locale, viewport, diagnostics limits, and timeout limits.
Configuration fields serialize in canonical order and reject unknown, missing, duplicate, out-of-range, or machine-local values.
Keyboard/mouse and gamepad fixtures map to the same CS-003 device-independent intents.
verification: Automated: round-trip, canonical-order, same-seed, changed-seed, unknown-field, invalid-bound, and device-equivalence tests.
Manual/playable: load the same fixture in editor and a Windows smoke invocation and compare resolved setup.
non_goals: no device checks in gameplay code
tasks.handoff_notes: All later EH tasks consume this one configuration and seed fixture; do not fork per-run ad hoc settings.

tasks.title: Implement bounded local diagnostics and run-validity recording
tasks.summary: Create an evidence-only recorder for bounded local diagnostic events and explicit run-validity transitions.
tasks.objective: Separate technical invalidation from fun or behavior evidence while keeping logs local, rotated, redacted, and size-bounded.
tasks.context: Milestone S1.0 Foundation and evidence harness; frozen product question: Can Shooter Mover provide a reproducible, rapidly resettable Stage 1 evidence environment with build identity, deterministic setup, bounded local diagnostics, benchmark combat space, a short-route shell, quick restart, and trustworthy evidence capture before gameplay systems are implemented?
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity v1 and Diagnostics/RunValidity v1.
Consumed output: canonical EH-002 run configuration and bounds.
implementation_notes: Forbidden: accepted planning/requirements, assembly-generated task artifacts, credentials or secrets, unowned serialized assets, Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Domain/, Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/, and gameplay implementation. Keep diagnostics local/offline and bounded.
Serialized owner: tools/evidence/fixtures/run-validity-cases-v1.json only. Save and registry impact: none.
Events are append-only within a session, locally rotated, explicitly exportable, and free of secrets or unnecessary personal data.
Rollback: remove recorder/fixtures/docs; no gameplay or durable mission state may depend on this recorder. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: The recorder captures identity, session start/end/restart, diagnostic commands, exceptions, timeouts, missing assets, performance warnings, and validity reason codes using CS-012.
Bounds cap event count, payload size, file size, and retained rotations; overflow produces an explicit truncation/invalidity event rather than silent loss.
Technical invalidity is distinct from negative human feedback and cannot be cleared by later events.
verification: Automated: event-order, rotation, redaction, overflow, duplicate-start, end-without-start, invalidity-monotonicity, and malformed-event tests.
Manual/playable: inspect one valid and one intentionally invalid local session log without network access.
proof_required: Valid and invalid example diagnostic logs
tasks.handoff_notes: EH-006, EH-007, EH-008, and EH-010 consume this recorder and its explicit validity outcome.

tasks.title: Create the Stage 1 benchmark arena shell
tasks.summary: Create one test-owned additive 2D arena scene shell with deterministic spawn sockets, camera bounds, collision envelope, evidence markers, and empty combat hooks.
tasks.objective: Provide a reproducible benchmark combat space without implementing movement, weapons, enemies, rewards, or durable room truth.
tasks.context: Milestone S1.0 Foundation and evidence harness; frozen product question: Can Shooter Mover provide a reproducible, rapidly resettable Stage 1 evidence environment with build identity, deterministic setup, bounded local diagnostics, benchmark combat space, a short-route shell, quick restart, and trustworthy evidence capture before gameplay systems are implemented?
inputs: Consumed foundation: composition-root shell, Bootstrap scene, and FoundationSmoke scene.
Consumed output: canonical EH-002 evidence configuration.
implementation_notes: Forbidden: accepted planning/requirements, assembly-generated task artifacts, credentials or secrets, unowned serialized assets, Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Domain/, Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/, and gameplay implementation. Keep diagnostics local/offline and bounded.
Serialized owner: Stage1BenchmarkArena.unity only. Save/content/registry impact: none.
The scene contains placeholders and stable test markers only; it must not own permanent room, encounter, reward, or progression truth.
Rollback: delete the owned scene, fixture, test, and document. Prototype debt: any placeholder visual is test-only and not release content.
acceptance_criteria: The scene loads additively from the UF-007 bootstrap path and exposes stable named sockets for player, targets, hazards, camera bounds, and performance probes.
Two resets produce the same object graph, transforms, sorting setup, and evidence marker IDs.
The shell is fully 2D and contains no gameplay behavior, remote dependency, unowned prefab, or direct scene search requirement.
verification: Automated: additive load/unload, marker uniqueness, transform snapshot, camera/collision bounds, missing-socket, and second-reset equality tests.
Manual/playable: open the arena through the evidence configuration and review scale, empty readability, and reset behavior.
tasks.handoff_notes: EH-005 may reuse documented socket conventions but owns its own scene; later gameplay batches project into this shell without editing its evidence markers.

tasks.title: Create the Stage 1 short-route shell
tasks.summary: Create a test-owned additive short-route scene shell connecting start, arena, transition, restart, and completion-review markers through room-projection interfaces.
tasks.objective: Provide deterministic multi-space lifecycle coverage before route gameplay, encounters, mission state, rewards, or persistence are implemented.
tasks.context: Milestone S1.0 Foundation and evidence harness; frozen product question: Can Shooter Mover provide a reproducible, rapidly resettable Stage 1 evidence environment with build identity, deterministic setup, bounded local diagnostics, benchmark combat space, a short-route shell, quick restart, and trustworthy evidence capture before gameplay systems are implemented?
inputs: Consumed foundation: composition root, Bootstrap, and FoundationSmoke scene.
Consumed contract: Room Projection v1.
Consumed outputs: canonical evidence configuration and arena socket conventions.
implementation_notes: Forbidden: accepted planning/requirements, assembly-generated task artifacts, credentials or secrets, unowned serialized assets, Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Domain/, Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/, and gameplay implementation. Keep diagnostics local/offline and bounded.
Serialized owner: Stage1ShortRouteShell.unity only. Save/content/registry impact: none.
Route nodes are projections/test markers; no scene object may assert durable clear, objective, checkpoint, reward, or completion truth.
Rollback: delete owned scene/fixture/test/document. Prototype debt: test-only placeholder geometry is allowed only inside this scene.
acceptance_criteria: The shell declares stable start, arena-entry, connector, restart, and review-end markers plus deterministic additive load/unload order.
Repeated enter, leave, unload, and reload operations are idempotent through CS-007 room lifecycle semantics.
The scene contains no direct object references across additive-room boundaries and no Stage 2 route implementation.
verification: Automated: marker uniqueness, lifecycle order, repeated load/unload, interrupted unload, missing connection, and projection-read-only tests.
Manual/playable: traverse the empty shell using test controls and confirm clear spatial orientation and rapid return to start.
tasks.handoff_notes: EH-006 owns restart/session behavior across this route; later route gameplay consumes CS-007 and must not make this shell authoritative.

tasks.title: Implement rapid reset, restart, and session lifecycle harness
tasks.summary: Create the evidence-session controller that starts from the canonical fixture, resets arena/route state, performs quick restart, and closes sessions cleanly.
tasks.objective: Guarantee rapidly repeatable evidence attempts without retaining hidden state or relying on gameplay systems.
tasks.context: Milestone S1.0 Foundation and evidence harness; frozen product question: Can Shooter Mover provide a reproducible, rapidly resettable Stage 1 evidence environment with build identity, deterministic setup, bounded local diagnostics, benchmark combat space, a short-route shell, quick restart, and trustworthy evidence capture before gameplay systems are implemented?
inputs: Consumed contracts: Player Intents v1 and Diagnostics/RunValidity v1.
Consumed outputs: configuration, diagnostics recorder, arena shell, and route shell.
implementation_notes: Forbidden: accepted planning/requirements, assembly-generated task artifacts, credentials or secrets, unowned serialized assets, Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Domain/, Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/, and gameplay implementation. Keep diagnostics local/offline and bounded.
Serialized owner: none. Save/content/registry impact: none.
Restart may recreate test projections and clear harness-local state only; it must not define future MissionRunState, save, reward, or checkpoint semantics.
Rollback: remove owned support/test/docs and use manual scene reload. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: A session has explicit configured, starting, running, restarting, ending, ended, and invalid states with rejected illegal transitions.
Quick restart returns to the same canonical start state and identity/configuration while issuing a new attempt ID and preserving the parent session audit trail.
Fifty consecutive reset/restart cycles leave no duplicate markers, retained subscriptions, leaked test objects, or stale intent state.
verification: Automated: legal/illegal transitions, restart latency bound, focus-loss intent clear, 50-cycle leak sentinel, interrupted restart, and end-idempotency tests.
Manual/playable: perform repeated arena and short-route restarts and confirm immediate predictable recovery.
tasks.handoff_notes: EH-009 invokes this lifecycle from edit/play/Windows smoke entrypoints; later gameplay tasks plug into it rather than replacing its evidence semantics.

tasks.title: Add representative performance capture hooks
tasks.summary: Add evidence-only frame-time, allocation, object-count, loading, and marker hooks with bounded capture windows and explicit warm-up.
tasks.objective: Make later representative combat performance measurable without changing simulation, quality rules, or gameplay code.
tasks.context: Milestone S1.0 Foundation and evidence harness; frozen product question: Can Shooter Mover provide a reproducible, rapidly resettable Stage 1 evidence environment with build identity, deterministic setup, bounded local diagnostics, benchmark combat space, a short-route shell, quick restart, and trustworthy evidence capture before gameplay systems are implemented?
inputs: Consumed contract: Diagnostics/RunValidity v1.
Consumed outputs: bounded recorder, arena shell, and short-route shell.
Consumed policy: provisional Windows performance acceptance.
implementation_notes: Forbidden: accepted planning/requirements, assembly-generated task artifacts, credentials or secrets, unowned serialized assets, Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Domain/, Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/, and gameplay implementation. Keep diagnostics local/offline and bounded.
Serialized owner: tools/evidence/fixtures/performance-budget-stage1-v1.json only. Save/content/registry impact: none.
Hooks observe and annotate; they never alter time scale, simulation, collision, quality behavior, or scene contents to improve results.
Rollback: remove owned files and performance annotations. Prototype debt: thresholds are versioned provisional evidence values, not hidden tuning.
acceptance_criteria: The probe records warm-up, capture duration, frame-time percentiles, managed allocations, scene-load duration, memory, and configured object-budget counters.
Capture windows and payloads are bounded and emit CS-012 warnings/invalidity when samples are incomplete, clock-invalid, or overrun.
Quality-profile changes are recorded and cannot change hidden game rules.
verification: Automated: percentile calculation, warm-up exclusion, zero-sample, clock reversal, allocation bound, object counter, and quality-identity tests.
Manual/playable: capture empty arena and short-route baselines and inspect locally exported summaries.
tasks.handoff_notes: EH-008 packages these summaries; later combat and reliability batches populate the same hooks with representative workloads.

tasks.title: Generate immutable evidence manifests and checksums
tasks.summary: Create a deterministic offline evidence-manifest generator binding identity, configuration, validity, diagnostics, performance summaries, file inventory, and SHA-256 checksums.
tasks.objective: Make every captured evidence package reviewable, immutable, and detectably incomplete or modified.
tasks.context: Milestone S1.0 Foundation and evidence harness; frozen product question: Can Shooter Mover provide a reproducible, rapidly resettable Stage 1 evidence environment with build identity, deterministic setup, bounded local diagnostics, benchmark combat space, a short-route shell, quick restart, and trustworthy evidence capture before gameplay systems are implemented?
inputs: Consumed contracts: Identity v1 and Diagnostics/RunValidity v1.
Consumed foundation: UF-010 Windows artifact entrypoint.
Consumed outputs: identity, configuration, diagnostics, validity, and performance summaries.
implementation_notes: Forbidden: accepted planning/requirements, assembly-generated task artifacts, credentials or secrets, unowned serialized assets, Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Domain/, Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/, and gameplay implementation. Keep diagnostics local/offline and bounded.
Serialized owner: evidence-manifest-v1.schema.json only. Save/content/registry impact: none.
Generated manifests are regenerate-only, canonical, path-normalized, machine-neutral, and exclude secrets, temporary files, and absolute local paths.
Rollback: remove generator/schema/tests/docs; existing evidence remains readable but is not accepted as formally manifested. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Identical evidence inputs yield byte-identical canonical manifests and checksums; changed, missing, extra, or reordered files are detected.
The manifest binds build/content/tuning identity, config/seed, session/attempt IDs, validity, diagnostics bounds, performance capture, tool version, and artifact inventory.
A failed or incomplete Windows build from UF-010 cannot be marked as a valid immutable artifact.
verification: Automated: canonical order, SHA-256 vector, path normalization, tamper, missing file, extra file, invalid session, and cross-directory reproducibility tests.
Manual/playable: generate two packages from copied inputs and compare byte-for-byte manifests offline.
edge_cases: checksum file included in its own digest
tasks.handoff_notes: EH-009 uses this generator for Windows smoke output; EH-010 validates and reviews manifested sessions.

tasks.title: Provide edit-mode, play-mode, and Windows smoke entrypoints
tasks.summary: Provide three explicit local entrypoints that run the evidence identity/configuration checks, scene/session smoke, and UF-010 Windows build smoke with manifested outputs.
tasks.objective: Make the Stage 1 evidence environment reproducible from clean commands without introducing CI, release, or gameplay implementation.
tasks.context: Milestone S1.0 Foundation and evidence harness; frozen product question: Can Shooter Mover provide a reproducible, rapidly resettable Stage 1 evidence environment with build identity, deterministic setup, bounded local diagnostics, benchmark combat space, a short-route shell, quick restart, and trustworthy evidence capture before gameplay systems are implemented?
inputs: Consumed foundation: UF-009 edit/play smoke coverage and UF-010 Windows build entrypoint.
Consumed outputs: session lifecycle and evidence manifest generator.
implementation_notes: Forbidden: accepted planning/requirements, assembly-generated task artifacts, credentials or secrets, unowned serialized assets, Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Domain/, Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/, and gameplay implementation. Keep diagnostics local/offline and bounded.
Serialized owner: none. Save/content/registry impact: none.
Scripts are local/offline, fail fast, use repository-relative paths, preserve exit codes, and never modify accepted planning or task artifacts.
Rollback: remove owned scripts/test/docs and retain lower-level UF/EH tools. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Each entrypoint resolves the canonical config, captures identity, emits bounded diagnostics, records validity, and writes a manifested result to a caller-selected output directory.
Edit-mode and play-mode entrypoints exercise their intended test layers; Windows smoke invokes UF-010 and verifies the built artifact starts, loads the harness shell, restarts, exits, and manifests.
Missing Unity/editor/build prerequisites produce a technical failure with actionable diagnostics, never a false pass.
verification: Automated: argument parsing, repository-relative paths, nonzero-exit propagation, stale-output rejection, manifest creation, and entrypoint integration tests.
Manual/playable: run all three documented commands on Windows and inspect separate valid/failed output examples.
proof_required: Manifest from a successful Windows smoke
edge_cases: build starts but harness does not load
tasks.handoff_notes: EH-010 is the review/validity gate for these outputs; later batches may call the entrypoints but may not weaken their failure semantics.

tasks.title: Define the Stage 1 evidence protocol and invalid-session review
tasks.summary: Define and validate the operator/human-review protocol for evidence preparation, execution, invalid-session classification, technical rerun, and shell review.
tasks.objective: Turn harness output into trustworthy review evidence without treating technical failures as negative fun votes or claiming gameplay proof.
tasks.context: Milestone S1.0 Foundation and evidence harness; frozen product question: Can Shooter Mover provide a reproducible, rapidly resettable Stage 1 evidence environment with build identity, deterministic setup, bounded local diagnostics, benchmark combat space, a short-route shell, quick restart, and trustworthy evidence capture before gameplay systems are implemented?
inputs: Consumed contract: Diagnostics/RunValidity v1.
Consumed outputs: diagnostics, session lifecycle, immutable manifest, and all smoke entrypoints.
Consumed milestone rule: invalid sessions are reliability failures and are rerun.
implementation_notes: Forbidden: accepted planning/requirements, assembly-generated task artifacts, credentials or secrets, unowned serialized assets, Assets/ShooterMover/Runtime/Domain/, Assets/ShooterMover/ContentPackages/, and gameplay implementation. Keep diagnostics local/offline and bounded.
Serialized owner: none. Save/content/registry impact: none.
The validator reads manifested evidence only and never repairs, edits, reclassifies, or uploads a session automatically.
Rollback: remove validator/tests/docs; no evidence round may proceed without an approved replacement protocol. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: The protocol fixes preparation, identity/config checks, allowed diagnostic commands, restart handling, required artifacts, human shell-review questions, and sign-off fields.
Invalid-session reasons are explicit, monotonic, traceable to CS-012, and separated from behavior/fun observations; invalid technical sessions require rerun.
The validator rejects checksum drift, missing proof, conflicting validity, unmanifested files, unsupported protocol versions, and incomplete human review.
verification: Automated: valid package, checksum drift, missing proof, conflicting validity, unsupported version, duplicate review, and invalid-session classification tests.
Manual/playable: independently review one valid shell session and one intentionally invalid session using only the package and documents.
edge_cases: diagnostic command used during frozen run
tasks.handoff_notes: Human review stops here. The next guided batch is movement-thruster only after this task batch is accepted; no gameplay implementation is assigned by this decomposition step.
